2605.10840.title: Clin-JEPA: A Multi-Phase Co-Training Framework for Joint-Embedding Predictive Pretraining on EHR Patient Trajectories
2605.10840.paper_summary: We present Clin-JEPA, a multi-phase co-training framework for joint-embedding predictive (JEPA) pretraining on EHR patient trajectories. JEPA architectures have enabled latent-space planning in robotics and high-quality representation learning in vision, but extending the paradigm to EHR data -- to obtain a single backbone that simultaneously forecasts patient trajectories and serves diverse downstream risk-prediction tasks without per-task fine-tuning -- remains an open challenge. Existing JEPA frameworks either discard the predictor after pretraining (I-JEPA, V-JEPA) or train it on a frozen pretrained encoder (V-JEPA 2-AC), leaving the encoder unaware of the rollout signal that the retained predictor must use at inference; co-training the encoder and predictor under a shared JEPA prediction objective would supply this grounding, but naïve co-training is unstable, with representation collapse and online/target drift causing autoregressive rollout to diverge. Clin-JEPA's five-phase pretraining curriculum -- predictor warmup, joint refinement, EMA target alignment, hard sync, and predictor finalization -- addresses each failure mode by phase, stably co-training a Qwen3-8B-based encoder and a 92M-parameter latent trajectory predictor. On MIMIC-IV ICU data, three independent evaluations support the framework: (1) latent $\ell_1$ rollout drift uniquely converges ($-$15.7%) over 48-hour horizons while baselines and ablations diverge (+3% to +4951%); (2) the encoder learns a clinically discriminative latent geometry (deteriorating-patient cohorts displace 4.83$\times$ further than stable patients in latent space, vs $\leq$2.62$\times$ for baseline encoders); (3) a single backbone outperforms strong tabular and sequence baselines on multi-task downstream evaluation. Clin-JEPA achieves mean AUROC 0.851 on ICareFM EEP and 0.883 on 8 binary risk tasks (+0.038 and +0.041 vs baseline average).
2605.10840.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了Clin-JEPA，一個多階段共同訓練框架，用於對電子健康記錄（EHR）患者軌跡進行聯合嵌入預測（JEPA）預訓練。JEPA架構已在機器人技術中實現了潛在空間規劃，並在視覺中實現了高品質的表示學習，但將這一範式擴展到EHR數據——以獲得一個同時預測患者軌跡並服務於多樣化下游風險預測任務的單一骨幹，而無需針對每個任務進行微調——仍然是一個未解的挑戰。現有的JEPA框架要麼在預訓練後丟棄預測器（I-JEPA，V-JEPA），要麼在凍結的預訓練編碼器上訓練它（V-JEPA 2-AC），使得編碼器無法感知保留的預測器在推理時必須使用的回放信號；在共享JEPA預測目標下共同訓練編碼器和預測器將提供這一基礎，但幼稚的共同訓練是不穩定的，表示崩潰和在線/目標漂移導致自回歸回放發散。Clin-JEPA的五階段預訓練課程——預測器熱身、聯合精煉、EMA目標對齊、硬同步和預測器最終化——針對每個失效模式進行了階段性處理，穩定地共同訓練基於Qwen3-8B的編碼器和一個92M參數的潛在軌跡預測器。在MIMIC-IV ICU數據上，三個獨立的評估支持該框架：（1）潛在的$\ell_1$回放漂移在48小時的範圍內獨特地收斂（$-$15.7%），而基準和消融實驗則發散（+3%到+4951%）；（2）編碼器學習到了一種臨床可區分的潛在幾何形狀（惡化患者群體在潛在空間中比穩定患者位移4.83$\times$更遠，而基準編碼器則不超過$\leq$2.62$\times$）；（3）單一骨幹在多任務下游評估中超越了強大的表格和序列基準。Clin-JEPA在ICareFM EEP上實現了平均AUROC 0.851，在8個二元風險任務上實現了0.883（比基準平均值高出+0.038和+0.041）。
2605.10840.author: Yixuan Yang et.al.
2605.10840.authors: Yixuan Yang, Mehak Arora, Ryan Zhang, Baraa Abed, Junseob Kim, Tilendra Choudhary, Md Hassanuzzaman, Kevin Zhu, Ayman Ali, Chengkun Yang, Alasdair Edward Gent, Victor Moas, Rishikesan Kamaleswaran
